counters.description: why: kindred’s mobility allows her to dodge sejuani’s abilities, and her r (lamb’s respite) can negate sejuani’s kill potential in ganks or teamfights.

why: olaf’s r (ragnarok) makes him immune to sejuani’s crowd control, allowing him to ignore her cc and chase her down easily. his sustain and true damage from q (reckless swing) can quickly whittle sejuani down in extended fights.

why: trundle’s r (subjugate) steals sejuani’s resistances, making her much less tanky during fights. his q (chomp) reduces her attack damage, and he can zone her out of objectives with w (frozen domain).
fiddlesticks can poke from a distance and fear sejuani when she tries to engage. his ultimate can counter her strong teamfighting ability by dealing massive aoe damage
why: graves has high mobility and burst damage, making it hard for sejuani to lock him down. his ranged autos and e (quickdraw) allow him to kite her easily.

synergies.description: teemo
get master yi to dive onto the carry while you cook em from the front.

zed is an assassin who focuses on burst damage and quickly eliminating backline targets. sejuani’s engage and lockdown aren't optimal for a champion like zed, who prefers isolated duels rather than extended teamfights​
why: sejuani’s cc allows katarina to safely dive into the enemy team and unleash her damage without being interrupted.
jhin’s playstyle revolves around staying far from the frontline and poking. he can’t always follow up on sejuani’s engages effectively, especially in mobile, fast-paced teamfights​
why: sejuani’s aoe cc from r (glacial prison) locks down multiple enemies, making it easy for miss fortune to channel r (bullet time) for massive aoe damage.

why: jinx excels at dealing damage from a distance, and sejuani’s crowd control helps keep enemies away from jinx while she gets excited and ramps up her dps. sejuani’s r (glacial prison) is perfect for keeping enemies locked in place, allowing jinx to free-fire.
why: sejuani’s knockups with q (arctic assault) and r (glacial prison) perfectly set up yasuo’s r (last breath). together, they can chain cc to wipe out the enemy team.
why: ryze’s burst damage and aoe spells benefit greatly from sejuani’s ability to lock down enemies. sejuani can hold targets in place long enough for ryze to combo his damage effectively.

why: vayne deals a lot of single-target damage and benefits from sejuani’s cc to help peel for her in teamfights. however, sejuani’s ability to engage and lock down enemies isn’t always fully utilized by vayne’s playstyle, which relies more on kiting.
why: sejuani can bring orianna’s ball into the enemy team for a devastating r (shockwave). however, orianna’s positioning can sometimes make this combo harder to execute perfectly.

why: ezreal plays from a safe distance and relies on poke, which doesn’t always capitalize on sejuani’s hard engage. while he can follow up with his r (trueshot barrage), the synergy isn’t as strong as with other carries.
why: while both sejuani and ashe have strong crowd control, their playstyles don’t always align perfectly. ashe prefers to poke and kite, while sejuani looks to dive in and disrupt.
why: kog’maw is a hyper-scaling adc with minimal mobility. while sejuani can peel for kog’maw, his lack of early-game synergy and reliance on scaling doesn’t align well with sejuani’s need to engage.
